articles.title: Odontogenic cystic lesion segmentation on cone-beam CT using an auto-adapting multi-scaled UNet
articles.authors: Huang Z; Li B; Cheng Y; Kim J
articles.keywords: CBCT; UNet; segmentation; odontogenic cyst; ameloblastoma; deep learning
articles.abstract: Odontogenic Cystic Lesions (OCLs) are common jaw pathologies including radicular cysts, dentigerous cysts, OKC, and ameloblastoma. OCL-Net, combining a multi-scale module with auto-adapting ROI, was proposed for automatic segmentation of OCLs in CBCT. Compared to standard UNet with multi-scale modules, OCL-Net achieved superior results. Segmented 3D masks can improve dental diagnostic efficiency for OCLs. The model demonstrated robust performance across subtypes including the most aggressive lesions.
